title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA combined quantization, caching, and step distillation to accelerate diffusion model inference from 50 steps down to 1-8 steps, enabling near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200. The approach includes dynamic quantization with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2, latent chunk caching to skip unchanged computations between denoising steps, and distribution-based training where student models match teacher output distributions.
writeup: Ziv Ilan presents how an NVIDIA team cut diffusion model inference from 50 steps to 1-8 steps by combining three techniques: quantization, caching, and step distillation. The methods include dynamic quantization developed with Black Forest Labs on Flux 2, latent chunk caching that skips unchanged computations between denoising steps, and distribution-based training where student models match teacher output distributions. The result enables near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200. The takeaway is that step reduction plus quantization and caching compound to make diffusion inference dramatically faster without separate retraining for each technique.
